description: Create Scanpy Quality Control Plots to enable unblinded dataset filtering with ScanpyUtilities
author: Anthony S. Castanza
plan:
## ScanpyQC GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
ScanpyQC is a GenePattern module that Create Scanpy Quality Control Plots to enable unblinded dataset filtering with ScanpyUtilities

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 4 parameters:
- Required: data.file, output.basename
- Optional: mtGenes, genome

### 3. Command Line
```
bash /scanpyqc/run_module.sh --python.executable\=python --src.path\=/scanpyqc/ --data.file\=<data.file> <mtGenes> --output.basename\=<output.basename> <genome>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: genepattern/scanpyqc\:0.100

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: data.file
parameters.description: A file containing single-cell data. h5ad, loom and mtx file formats are accepted. A 10x formatted hdf5 file may also be provided along with the 'genome' argument. The file will be internally converted to an h5ad file before proceeding.

parameters.name: mtGenes
parameters.description: A file containing a list of the gene ids for mitochondrial genes. Optional, used for filtering by mitochondrial abundance.

parameters.name: output.basename
parameters.description: Base filename used for labeling output files

parameters.name: genome
parameters.description: When converting a 10x formated HDF5 file, the module will internally call read_10x_h5 from the scanpy package. This function expects a genome argument which specifies the name of the data set in the HDF5 file. e.g. 'GRCh38'
